LEVEL_1.description:
Niraparib, an orally available, small molecule PARP inhibitor, and abiraterone acetate, a CYP17 inhibitor, are FDA-approved in combination with prednisone for the treatment of adult patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious BRCA-mutated metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC), based on an FDA-approved test. BRCA1 mutations for treatment with niraparib and abiraterone acetate plus prednisone were detected with the FoundationOne CDx and FoundationOne Liquid CDx. FDA approval was based on the results of the Phase III MAGNITUDE (NCT03748641) and Phase III AMPLITUDE (NCT04497844) trials of niraparib and abiraterone acetate plus prednisone in patients with homologous recombination repair (HRR) gene-mutated mCRPC. 
In the Phase III MAGNITUDE (NCT03748641) trial for patients with BRCA1/2 mutations, the niraparib and abiraterone acetate plus prednisone cohort (n=113) demonstrated a median radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS) of 16.6 months (95% CI=13-NE) versus 10.9 months (95% CI = 8.3-13.8) in the placebo and abiraterone acetate plus prednisone cohort (n=112) (HR=0.53 [95% CI=0.36-0.79]; p<0.0014) (PMID: 36952634). In an exploratory overall survival (OS) analysis of the BRCA1/2 mutant subgroups, the niraparib cohort demonstrated a median OS of 30.4 months (95% CI=27.6-NE) versus 28.6 months (95% CI=23.8-33.0) (HR=0.79 (95% CI=0.55-1.12) (PMID: 36952634).
In the Phase III AMPLITUDE (NCT04497844) trial for patients with BRCA1/2 mutations, the niraparib and abiraterone acetate plus prednisone cohort (n=191 [BRCA1: n=25; BRCA2: n=148; co-occurring BRCA alterations: n=18]) demonstrated a median rPFS that was not evaluable (95% CI=41.2-NE) and a median OS that was also not evaluable (95% CI=NE-NE) (PMID: 41057655). The abiraterone acetate plus prednisone cohort (n=196 [BRCA1: n=25; BRCA2: n=144; co-occurring BRCA alterations: n=27]) demonstrated a median rPFS of 26.0 months (95% CI=22.1-41.2) (HR=0.52 [95% CI=0.37-0.72]; p<0.0001) and a median OS that was not evaluable (95% CI=36.2-NE) (HR=0.75 [95% CI=0.51-1.11]; p=0.15) (PMID: 41057655).

LEVEL_1.description:
Selpercatinib is a small molecule kinase inhibitor of the RET tyrosine kinase receptor that is FDA-approved for adult and pediatric patients 2 years of age and older with advanced or metastatic medullary thyroid cancer (MTC) with a RET mutation, as detected by an FDA-approved test, who require systemic therapy. RET mutations (single nucleotide variants, multiple nucleotide variants and deletions) for treatment with selpercatinib were detected with the Oncomine Dx Target Test. FDA approval was based on the results of the Phase III LIBRETTO-531 (NCT04211337) trial of selpercatinib versus physicians' choice of cabozantinib or vandetanib in adult and adolescent patients with advanced or metastatic RET-mutant MTC, including patients with germline pathogenic variants.
In the Phase III LIBRETTO-531 (NCT04211337), the selpercatinib treatment group (n=193 [n=121, RET M918T; n=72, RET other mutations]) demonstrated an overall response rate(ORR) of 69.4% (95% CI=62.4-75.8), with a 57.5% partial response (PR) rate, 11.9% complete response (CR) rate, 20.2% stable disease (SD) rate and 2.1% progressive disease (PD) rate, and a median PFS that was not reached (PMID: 37870969). The control treatment group (n=98  [n=61, RET M918T; n=37, RET other mutations]) demonstrated an ORR of 38.8% (95% CI=29.1-49.2), with a 34.7% PR rate, 4.1% CR rate, 49.0% SD rate and 1.0% PD rate, and a median PFS of 16.8 months (95% CI=12.2-25.1) (HR=0.28 [95% CI=0.16-0.48]; p=0.001) (PMID: 37870969).

articles.title: Phase 3 Trial of Selpercatinib in Advanced RET-Mutant Medullary Thyroid Cancer.

LEVEL_1.description:
Olaparib is an orally available, small molecule PARP inhibitor indicated in patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious germline-BRCA mutated, HER2-negative breast cancer human who have previously been treated with chemotherapy in the neoadjuvant, adjuvant or metastatic setting.  FDA approval was based on the Phase III OlympiAD trial (NCT02000622) of single agent olaparib versus standard of care based on physician's choice in 302 patients with germline-BRCA mutated, HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer who had received no more than two previous chemotherapy regimens. FDA approval in the adjuvant setting for patients with germline-BRCA mutated high-risk early-stage breast cancer was based on the results of the Phase III OlympiA (NCT02032823) trial of olaparib versus placebo in patients with germline-BRCA mutated, HER2-negative early-stage breast cancer.

In the Phase III OlympiAD trial (NCT02000622), the olaparib cohort (n=205 (n=117, germline BRCA1 mutations; n=84, germline BRCA2 mutations; n=4, germline BRCA1/2 mutations])  demonstrated a median progression-free survival (PFS) of 7.0 months (95% CI=0.43-0.80), a confirmed objective response rate (ORR) of 59.9% (95%CI=52.0-67.4), and a complete response (CR) rate of 9.0%. The standard therapy cohort (n=97 [n=51, germline BRCA1 mutations; n=46, germline BRCA2 mutations]) demonstrated a median PFS of 4.2 months (95% CI=0.43-0.80), a confirmed ORR of 28.8% (95%CI= 18.3-41.3), and a CR rate of 1.5%. Overall survival did not differ significantly between the groups (HR 0.90 [95%CI=0.63-1.29]) (PMID: 28578601).

In the Phase III OlympiA (NCT02032823) trial, the olaparib cohort (n=921 [n=657, germline BRCA1 mutations; n=261, germline BRCA2 mutations; n=2, germline BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutations; n=1, missing data]) had 85.9% of patients alive and free of invasive disease at three years versus 77.1% in the placebo cohort (n=915 [n=670, germline BRCA1 mutations; n=239, germline BRCA2 mutations; n=5, germline BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutations; n=1, missing data]) (difference=8.8% [95% CI=4.5-13.0]) (PMID: 34081848). Invasive disease-free survival was significantly longer in the olaparib cohort versus the placebo cohort (HR=0.58 [95% CI=0.41-0.821]; p<0.001) (PMID: 34081848).

articles.title: Olaparib for Metastatic Breast Cancer in Patients with a Germline BRCA Mutation.

LEVEL_1.description:
Everolimus is a small molecule inhibitor of mTOR that is FDA-approved for pediatric and adult patients with tuberous sclerosis complex (TSC) who have subependymal giant cell astrocytoma (SEGA) that requires therapeutic intervention but cannot be curatively resected. FDA approval is based on the results of the Phase III EXIST-1 (NCT00789828) trial of everolimus versus placebo for 117 pediatric and adult patients with TSC-associated SEGA. 
In the Phase III EXIST-1 (NCT00789828) trial, the everolimus cohort (n=78 [TSC1 and TSC2 mutated, n=2; TSC1 mutated, n=10; TSC2 mutated= 55; neither, n=11]) (median age: 9.5 years [range=1.0-23.9]) demonstrated a response rate of 35% (95% CI=24-46) and a 6-month progression-free survival (PFS) of 100% (PMID: 23158522). The placebo cohort (n=39 [TSC1 mutated, n=3; TSC2 mutated= 29; neither, n=7]) (median age: 7.1 years [range=0.8-26.6]) demonstrated a response rate of 0% (95% CI=0-9), leading to a difference in response rate of 34.62% (95% CI=15.10-52.37) (p<0.001), and a 6-month PFS of 86% (PMID: 23158522).

articles.title: Efficacy and safety of everolimus for subependymal giant cell astrocytomas associated with tuberous sclerosis complex (EXIST-1): a multicentre, randomised, placebo-controlled phase 3 trial.
articles.journal: Lancet (London, England)
articles.reference: Franz DN et al. Lancet (London, England). 2013 Jan 12;381(9861)125-32.

LEVEL_1.description:
Rucaparib is an orally available, small-molecule PARP inhibitor that is FDA-approved for the treatment of adult patients with a deleterious BRCA mutation (germline and/or somatic)-associated metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC), as detected by an FDA-approved companion diagnostic, who have been treated with androgen receptor-directed therapy and a taxane-based chemotherapy or androgen receptor-directed therapy alone. BRCA2 mutations for treatment with rucaparib were detected by the FoundationOne Liquid CDx. FDA approval was based on the results of the Phase III TRITON3 (NCT02975934) trial of rucaparib in 405 eligible patients with mCRPC pre-treated with androgen receptor-directed therapy who could not have received prior chemotherapy in the castration-resistant setting.
In the Phase III TRITON3 (NCT02975934) trial of rucaparib versus physician's choice in patients with pre-treated BRCA-mutated mCRPC, patients treated with rucaparib (n=201) demonstrated a median progression-free survival of 11.2 months (95% CI=9.2-13.8) compared to 6.4 months (95% CI=5.4-8.3) for patients treated with physician's choice (n=101) (HR=0.05 [95% CI=0.36-0.69]) (PMID: 36795891). In addition, patients treated with rucaparib (n=201) demonstrated a median overall survival of 23.2 months (95% CI=19.1-25.2) compared to 21.2 months (95% CI= 18.0-23.1) for patients treated with physician's choice (n=101) (HR=0.91 [95% CI=0.68-1.20]) (PMID: 36795891).

articles.title: Rucaparib or Physician's Choice in Metastatic Prostate Cancer.

LEVEL_1.description:
Olaparib is an orally available, small molecule PARP inhibitor that is FDA-approved for patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious germline or somatic homologous recombination repair (HRR) gene-mutated metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC), who have progressed following prior treatment with enzalutamide or abiraterone. BRIP1 deleterious mutations for treatment with olaparib were detected by FoundationOne CDx or FoundationOne Liquid CDx. FDA approval was based on the results of the Phase III PROfound (NCT02987543) trial of olaparib versus enzalutamide or abiraterone acetate in adult patients with mCRPC who had disease progression while receiving a new hormonal agent and who had a deleterious or suspected deleterious mutation in a homologous recombination repair gene. 
In the Phase III PROfound (NCT02987543) trial, Cohort B (n=387 [BRCA2: n=127; ATM: n=84; BRCA1: n=13; CDK12: n=89; CHEK2: n=12; PPP2R2A: n=10; RAD51B: n=5; RAD54L: n=5; PALB2: n=4; BRIP1: n=3; CHEL1: n=2; BARD1: n=1; RAD515D: n=1; Co-occurring mutations: n=7]) treated with olaparib (n=256) demonstrated a median radiological progression-free survival (PFS) of 5.8 months (95% CI=5.5-7.4) (PMID: 32343890). In the enzalutamide or abiraterone acetate treated Cohort B (n=131), the median radiological PFS was 3.5 months (95% CI=2.2-3.7) (HR=00.49 [95% CI=0.38-0.63]; p<0.0001) (PMID: 32343890).

LEVEL_1.description:
Talazoparib is an orally available, small molecule PARP inhibitor that is FDA-approved for patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious germline BRCA-mutated, HER2-negative locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer. FDA approval was based on the results from the Phase III EMBRACA (NCT01945775) trial of talazoparib versus physician's choice (standard-therapy) in 431 patients with advanced, BRCA1/2 germline-mutated breast cancer.

In the Phase III EMBRACA (NCT01945775) trial, the talazoparib cohort (n=287 [n=133, germline BRCA1 mutations; n=154, germline BRCA2 mutations]) demonstrated a median progression-free survival (PFS) of 8.6 months (95% CI=7.2-9.3), a median overall survival (OS) of 22.3 months (95% CI=18.1-26.2) and an objective response rate (ORR) of 62.6% (95% CI=55.8-69.0), with a 5.5% (n=12) complete response (CR) rate, 57.1% (n=125) partial response (PR) rate and 21.0% (n=46) stable disease (SD) rate (PMID: 30110579). In the physician's choice cohort (n=144 [n=63, germline BRCA1 mutations; n=81, germline BRCA2 mutations]), the median PFS was 5.6 months (95% CI=4.2-6.7) (HR=0.54 [95% CI=0.41-0.71]; p<0.0001), the median OS was 19.5 months (95% CI=16.3-22.4) (HR=0.76 [95% CI=0.55-1.06]; p=0.105) and the ORR was 27.2% (95% CI=19.3-36.3), with a 27.2% (n=31) PR rate and 31.6% (n=36) SD rate (PMID: 30110579).

articles.title: Talazoparib in Patients with Advanced Breast Cancer and a Germline BRCA Mutation.

LEVEL_1.description:
Olaparib is an orally available, small molecule PARP inhibitor that is FDA approved for the maintenance treatment of adult patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious germline-BRCA mutated metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma whose disease has not progressed on at least 16 weeks of a first-line platinum-based chemotherapy regimen. FDA approval was based on the Phase III POLO (NCT02184195) trial of single agent olaparib versus placebo in 154 patients with germline-BRCA mutated metastatic pancreatic adenocarcinoma.

In the Phase III POLO (NCT02184195) trial, the olaparib cohort (n=92 [Germline BRCA1: n=29; Germline BRCA2: n=62; Germline BRCA1 and BRCA2: n=1]) demonstrated a median progression-free survival (PFS) of 7.4 months and a median overall survival (OS) of 18.9 months (PMID: 31157963). The placebo cohort (n=62 [Germline BRCA1: n=16; Germline BRCA2: n=46]) demonstrated a median PFS of 3.8 months (HR=0.53 [95% CI=0.35-0.82]; p=0.004) and a median OS of 18.1 months (HR=0.91 [95% CI=0.56-1.46]; p=0.68) (PMID: 31157963).

articles.title: Maintenance Olaparib for Germline BRCA-Mutated Metastatic Pancreatic Cancer.

LEVEL_1.description:
Mirdametinib is an orally available, small molecule MEK1/2 inhibitor that is FDA-approved for the treatment of adult and pediatric patients 2 years of age and older with neurofibromatosis type 1 (NF1) who have symptomatic plexiform neurofibromas (PN) not amenable to complete resection. FDA approval was based on the results of the Phase IIb ReNeu (NCT03962543) trial of mirdametinib in adult and pediatric patients with symptomatic NF1-associated plexiform neurofibroma.
In the Phase IIb ReNeu (NCT03962543) trial, the adult cohort (n=58 [age range=18-69 years old; median age=34 years old]) demonstrated an overall response rate (ORR) of 41% (95% CI=29-55) (p<0.001), with 24 patients demonstrating partial responses (PR) and 26 patients demonstrating stable disease (SD) (PMID: 39514826). In the pediatric cohort (n=56 [age range=2-17 years old; median age=10 years old]), the ORR was 52% (95% CI=38-65) (p<0.001), with 29 patients demonstrating PR and 22 patients demonstrating SD (PMID: 39514826).

articles.title: ReNeu: A Pivotal, Phase IIb Trial of Mirdametinib in Adults and Children With Symptomatic Neurofibromatosis Type 1-Associated Plexiform Neurofibroma.

Niraparib is an orally available, small molecule PARP inhibitor that is FDA-approved for the maintenance treatment of adult patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious germline BRCA-mutated recurrent epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube (FT) or primary peritoneal (PP) cancer who are in a complete or partial response to platinum-based chemotherapy or who are in a complete or partial response to first-line platinum-based chemotherapy and whose cancer is associated with homologous recombination deficiency (HRD)-positive status defined by either a deleterious or suspected deleterious BRCA mutation, and/or genomic instability. Deleterious or suspected deleterious germline BRCA mutations for treatment with niraparib were detected by the Myriad myChoice CDx. FDA approval was based on the results of the Phase III PRIMA (NCT02655016) as maintenance therapy for newly diagnosed germline BRCA-associated advanced ovarian/FT/PP cancer after response to frontline chemotherapy and Phase III NOVA (NCT01847274) as maintenance therapy for recurrent germline BRCA-associated ovarian/FT/PP cancer after response to platinum-based chemotherapy.

In the Phase III PRIMA (NCT02655016) trial, the HRD-positive niraparib cohort (n=247, all HRD; n=152, all HRD including BRCA-mutated) demonstrated a median overall survival (OS) of 71.9 months and a median progression-free survival (PFS) of 24.5 months (PMID: 39284381). The HRD-positive placebo cohort (n=126, all HRD; n=71, all HRD including BRCA-mutated) demonstrated a median OS of 69.8 months (HR=0.95 [95% CI=0.70-1.29]) and a median PFS of 11.2 months (HR=0.51 [95% CI=0.40-0.66]) (PMID: 39284381). Of patients with BRCA mutations, the niraparib cohort demonstrated median PFS of 30.1 months versus 11.5 months in placebo cohort (HR=0.43 [95% CI=0.31-0.59]) (PMID: 39284381). 

In the Phase III NOVA (NCT01847274) trial of patients with germline BRCA-associated ovarian/FT/PP cancer, the niraparib cohort (n=138) demonstrated a median PFS of 21.0 months versus 5.5 months in the placebo cohort (n=65) (HR=0.27 [95% CI=0.17-0.41]) (PMID: 27717299). Of patients with no germline BRCA mutations, the niraparib cohort (n=234) demonstrated a median PFS of 9.3 months versus 3.9 months in the placebo cohort (n=116) (HR=0.45 [95% CI=0.34-0.61]) (PMID: 27717299).

Talazoparib is an orally available, small molecule PARP inhibitor that is FDA-approved n combination with enzalutamide for the treatment of adult patients with homologous recombination repair (HRR) gene-mutated metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC). FDA approval was based on the results of the Phase III TALAPRO-2 (NCT03395197) trial of talazoparib plus enzalutamide in adult patients with asymptomatic or mildly symptomatic mCRPC harboring HRR gene alterations receiving ongoing androgen deprivation therapy. 
In the Phase III TALAPRO-2 (NCT03395197) trial, the HRR-deficient population treated with talazoparib plus enzalutamide (n=200 [ATM: n=47; ATR: n=3; BRCA1: n=11; BRCA2: n=62; CDK12: n=36; CHEK2: n=34; FANCA: n=4; MLH1: n=9; MRE11A: n=1; NBN: n=8; PALB2: n=9; RAD515C: n=2]) demonstrated a median radiographic progression-free survival (PFS) that was not evaluable, a median duration of response (DOR) of 20.3 months (95% CI=12.2-NR) and a prostate-specific antigen (PSA) ≥50% response of 86% (95% CI=81-91) (PMID: 38049622). The HRR-deficient population treated with placebo plus enzalutamide (n=199 [ATM: n=39; ATR: n=12; BRCA1: n=12; BRCA2: n=73; CDK12: n=39; CHEK2: n=37; FANCA: n=5; MLH1: n=1; MRE11A: n=2; NBN: n=3; PALB2: n=8; RAD515C: n=2]) demonstrated a median radiographic PFS of 13.8 months (HR=0.44 [95% CI=0.32-0.60]; p<0.0001), a median DOR of 14.8 months (95% CI=6.6-25.8) and a PSA ≥50% response of 63% (95% CI=56-70) (PMID: 38049622). 
Of patients harboring only PALB2 mutations (n=11), the talazoparib plus enzalutamide subpopulation (n=6) demonstrated a radiographic PFS that was not evaluable versus 8.6 months in the placebo plus enzalutamide subpopulation (n=5) (HR=0.56 [95% CI=0.12-2.51], p=0.44) (PMID: 37285865)

LEVEL_1.description:
Belzutifan is an orally available, small molecule HIF-2α inhibitor that is FDA-approved for the treatment of adult patients with von Hippel-Lindau (VHL) disease who require therapy for associated renal cell carcinoma (RCC), central nervous system (CNS) hemangioblastomas, or pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (pNET), not requiring immediate surgery. FDA approval was based on the Phase II LITESPARK-004 (NCT03401788) trial of belzutifan in patients with CNS hemangioblastomas associated with VHL with a confirmed germine VHL alteration. 
In the Phase II LITESPARK-004 (NCT03401788) trial, patients with CNS hemangioblastomas (with solid lesions and cystic components) (n=50) demonstrated a median time to response of 5.4 months (IQR=2.7-16.5), a median duration of response (DOR) that was not reached (95% CI=30.9-NR) and an objective response rate (ORR) of 44% (95% CI=30-59), with an 8% (n=4) complete response (CR) rate, 36% (n=18) partial response (PR) rate, 46% (n=24) stable disease (SD) rate and 6% (n=3) progressive disease (PD) rate (PMID: 39284337). In patients with CNS hemangioblastomas (with solid lesions only) (n=25), the median time to response aws 3.1 months (IQR=2.6-8.5), the median DOR was not reached (95% CI=33.8-NR) and the ORR was 76% (95% CI=55-91), with a 4% (n=1) CR rate, 72% (n=18) PR rate and 20% (n=5) SD rate (PMID: 39284337).

Everolimus is a small molecule inhibitor of mTOR that is FDA-approved for adult patients with renal angiomyolipoma and tuberous sclerosis complex (TSC), not requiring immediate surgery. FDA approval is based on the results of the Phase III EXIST-2 (NCT00790400) trial of everolimus versus placebo in patients with TSC-associated renal angiomyolipoma.
In the Phase III EXIST-2 (NCT00790400) trial, the everolimus cohort (n=79 [TSC, n=77; sporadic lymphangioleiomyomatosis, n=2; lymphangioleiomyomatosis, n=22]) demonstrated a response rate of 42% (95% CI=31-53) while the placebo cohort (n=39 [TSC, n=36; sporadic lymphangioleiomyomatosis, n=3; lymphangioleiomyomatosis, n=7]) demonstrated a response rate of 0% (95% CI=0-9) (Difference in response rate=42% [95% CI=24-58]; p<0.0001) (PMID: 23312829).

LEVEL_1.description:
Rucaparib is an orally available, small molecule PARP inhibitor that is FDA-approved for the maintenance treatment of adult patients with a deleterious BRCA mutation (germline and/or somatic)- associated recurrent epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal cancer who are in a complete or partial response to platinum-based chemotherapy. BRCA1 mutations for maintenance treatment with rucaparib were detected by the BRACAnalysis CDx or the FoundationFocus CDxBRCA Assay. FDA approval was based on the results of the Phase III ARIEL3 (NCT01968213) trial of rucaparib versus placebo in 564 patients with epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal cancer. Additionally, rucaparib is NCCN recommended as a first-line maintenance treatment in newly diagnosed patients with advanced ovarian cancer based on the results of the Phase III ATHENA-MONO (NCT03522246) trial of rucaparib versus placebo in patients with newly diagnosed BRCA mutated ovarian cancer.

In the Phase III ARIEL3 (NCT01968213) trial, the rucaparib cohort (n=375 [BRCA1 mutated, n=80; BRCA2 mutated, n=50; Germline BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutated, n=82; Somatic BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutated, n=40]) (epithelial ovarian cancer, n=312; fallopian tube cancer, n=32; primary peritoneal cancer, n=31) demonstrated a median OS of 45.9 months (95% CI-37.7-59.6) and a median time to disease progression on subsequent therapy or death (PFS2) of 26.1 months (95% CI=22.8-32.8) in the BRCA-mutated subgroup (PMID: 40580808). In the placebo cohort (n=189 [BRCA1 mutated, n=37; BRCA2 mutated, n=29; Germline BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutated, n=48; Somatic BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutated, n=16]) (epithelial ovarian cancer, n=159; fallopian tube cancer, n=10; primary peritoneal cancer, n=19; high-grade serous adenocarcinoma, n=1), the median OS was 47.8 months. (95% CI=43.2-55.8) (HR=0.83 [95% CI=0.58-1.19]) and the median PFS2 was 18.2 months (95% CI=15.7-24.4) (HR=0.67 [95% CI=0.48-0.94]) in the BRCA-mutated subgroup (PMID: 40580808).

In the Phase III ATHENA-MONO (NCT03522246) trial, the homologous recombination deficiency (HRD) population was treated with either rucaparib (n=185 [BRCA mutated, n=91; BRCA wildtype/loss of heterozygosity [LOH] high, n=94] or placebo (n=49 [BRCA mutated, n=24; BRCA wildtype/LOH high, n=25] (PMID: 35658487). Of the rucaparib cohort, the median progression-free survival (PFS) was 28.7 months (95% CI=23.0-NR) and the objective response rate (ORR) was 58.8% (95% CI=32.9-81.6), with a 58.8% (n=10) partial response (PR) rate, 35.3% (n=6) stable disease (SD) rate and 5.9% (n=1) progressive disease (PD) rate (PMID: 35658487). Of the placebo cohort, the median PFS was 11.3 months (95% CI=9.1-22.1) (HR=0.47 [95% CI=0.31-22.1]; p=0.0004) and the ORR was 20.0% (95% CI=0.5-71.6), with a 20% (n=1) PR rate, 40% (n=2) SD rate and 40% (n=2) PD rate (PMID: 35658487).

LEVEL_1.description:
Olaparib is an orally available, small molecule PARP inhibitor that is FDA-approved for patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious germline or somatic homologous recombination repair (HRR) gene-mutated metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC), who have progressed following prior treatment with enzalutamide or abiraterone. BRCA1 deleterious mutations for treatment with olaparib were detected by FoundationOne CDx or FoundationOne Liquid CDx. FDA approval was based on the results of the Phase III PROfound (NCT02987543) trial of olaparib versus enzalutamide or abiraterone acetate in adult patients with mCRPC who had disease progression while receiving a new hormonal agent and who had a deleterious or suspected deleterious mutation in a homologous recombination repair gene. 
In the Phase III PROfound (NCT02987543) trial, Cohort A (n=245 [n=127, BRCA2; n=84, ATM; n=13, BRCA1]) treated with olaparib (n=162) demonstrated a median radiological progression-free survival (PFS) of 7.4 months (95% CI=6.2-9.3), an overall response rate (ORR) of 33% (95% CI=23-45) and a median overall survival (OS) of 14.7 months (95% CI=11.9-18.8) (PMID: 32343890). In the enzalutamide or abiraterone acetate treated Cohort A (n=83), the median radiological PFS was 3.6 months (95% CI=1.9-3.7) (HR=0.34 [95% CI=0.25-0.47]; p<0.0001), the ORR was 2% (95% CI=0-12) and the median OS was 14.7 months (95% CI=11.9-18.8) (HR=0.69 [95% CI=0.50-0.97]; p=0.0175) (PMID: 32343890). 
In the germline post hoc analysis of the BRCA1/2 cohort (n=61) from the Phase III PROfound (NCT02987543) trial, the olaparib treated subpopulation (n=42) demonstrated a median radiological PFS of 10.4 months, an ORR of 47.4% and a median OS of 20.8 months (PMID: 37963304). In the enzalutamide or abiraterone acetate treated subpopulation (n=19), the median radiological PFS was 1.9 months (HR=0.08 [95% CI=0.03-0.18]), the ORR was 0% and the median OS was 15.1 months (HR=0.55 [95% CI=0.27-1.16]) (PMID: 37963304).

LEVEL_1.description:
Olaparib is an orally available, small molecule that is FDA-approved for the maintenance treatment of adult patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious germline or somatic BRCA-mutated advanced epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube or primary peritoneal cancer who are in complete or partial response to first-line platinum-based chemotherapy. Olaparib is additionally approved for the maintenance treatment of adult patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious germline or somatic BRCA-mutated recurrent epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube or primary peritoneal cancer, who are in complete or partial response to platinum-based chemotherapy. BRCA mutations for maintenance treatment with olaparib were detected with the BRACAnalysis CDx, FoundationOne CDx or the Myriad myChoice CDx. FDA approval was based on the results of the Phase III SOLO-1 (NCT01874353) and Phase III SOLO-2 (NCT01874353) trials of olaparib as maintenance treatment in patients with BRCA mutated epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube or primary peritoneal cancer following first-line platinum-based chemotherapy or who were in response to platinum-based therapy, respectively. 

In the Phase III SOLO-2 (NCT01874353) trial, the olaparib cohort (n=196) (ovarian cancer, n=162; fallopian tubes or primary peritoneal, n=31; other, n=2 other; missing data, n=1) demonstrated a median progression-free survival (PFS) of 19.1 months and a median overall survival (OS) of 51.7 months while the placebo cohort (n=99) (ovarian cancer, n=82; fallopian tubes or primary peritoneal, n=13) demonstrated a median PFS of 5.5 months (HR=0.30 [95% CI=0.22-0.41]; p<0.0001) and a median OS of 38.8 months (HR=0.74 [95% CI=0.54-1.00]; p=0.0537) (PMID: 33743851). Of germline BRCA mutated patients, the median OS was 52.4 months and 37.4 months (HR=0.71 [95% CI=0.52-0.97]; p=0.0306) in the olaparib and placebo cohorts, respectively (PMID: 33743851). 

In the Phase III SOLO-1 (NCT01874353) trial, the olaparib cohort (n=260 [BRCA1 mutation, n=191; BRCA2 mutation, n=66; BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutation, n=3]) demonstrated a median PFS that was not reached while the placebo cohort (n=131 [BRCA1 mutation, n=91; BRCA2 mutation, n=40]) demonstrated a median PFS of 13.8 months (HR=0.30 [95% CI=0.23-0.41]; p<0.0001) (PMID: 32749942).

LEVEL_1.description:
Olaparib is an orally available, small molecule that is FDA-approved for the maintenance treatment of adult patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious germline or somatic BRCA-mutated advanced epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube or primary peritoneal cancer who are in complete or partial response to first-line platinum-based chemotherapy. Olaparib is additionally approved for the maintenance treatment of adult patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious germline or somatic BRCA-mutated recurrent epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube or primary peritoneal cancer, who are in complete or partial response to platinum-based chemotherapy. BRCA mutations for maintenance treatment with olaparib were detected with the BRACAnalysis CDx, FoundationOne CDx or the Myriad myChoice CDx. FDA approval was based on the results of the Phase III SOLO-1 (NCT01874353) and Phase III SOLO-2 (NCT01874353) trials of olaparib as maintenance treatment in patients with BRCA mutated epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube or primary peritoneal cancer following first-line platinum-based chemotherapy or who were in response to platinum-based therapy, respectively. 

In the Phase III SOLO-2 (NCT01874353) trial, the olaparib cohort (n=196) (ovarian cancer, n=162; fallopian tubes or primary peritoneal, n=31; other, n=2 other; missing data, n=1) demonstrated a median progression-free survival (PFS) of 19.1 months and a median overall survival (OS) of 51.7 months while the placebo cohort (n=99) (ovarian cancer, n=82; fallopian tubes or primary peritoneal, n=13) demonstrated a median PFS of 5.5 months (HR=0.30 [95% CI=0.22-0.41]; p<0.0001) and a median OS of 38.8 months (HR=0.74 [95% CI=0.54-1.00]; p=0.0537) (PMID: 33743851). Of germline BRCA mutated patients, the median OS was 52.4 months and 37.4 months (HR=0.71 [95% CI=0.52-0.97]; p=0.0306) in the olaparib and placebo cohorts, respectively (PMID: 33743851). 

In the Phase III SOLO-1 (NCT01874353) trial, the olaparib cohort (n=260 [BRCA1 mutation, n=191; BRCA2 mutation, n=66; BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutation, n=3]) demonstrated a median PFS that was not reached while the placebo cohort (n=131 [BRCA1 mutation, n=91; BRCA2 mutation, n=40]) demonstrated a median PFS of 13.8 months (HR=0.30 [95% CI=0.23-0.41]; p<0.0001) (PMID: 32749942).

Olaparib is an orally available, small molecule PARP inhibitor that is FDA-approved with bevacizumab for the maintenance treatment of adult patients with advanced epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube (FT) or primary peritoneal (PP) cancer who are in complete or partial response to first-line platinum-based chemotherapy and whose cancer is associated with homologous recombination deficiency (HRD)-positive status defined by either a deleterious or suspected deleterious BRCA mutation, and/or genomic instability. Deleterious or suspected deleterious germline BRCA mutations for maintenance treatment with olaparib were detected with the BRACAnalysis CDx, FoundationOne CDx or the Myriad myChoice CDx. FDA approval was based on the Phase III PAOLA-1 (NCT02477644) trial of olaparib plus bevacizumab versus placebo plus bevacizumab in 806 patients with newly diagnosed advanced ovarian, FT or PP cancer (n=694, ovarian; n=50, FT; n=62, PP).

In the Phase III PAOLA-1 (NCT02477644) trial, the olaparib cohort (n=537 [n, 255, HRD-positive; n=157, BRCA mutation-positive; n=192, HRD-negative; n=90, HRD unknown]) demonstrated a median overall survival (OS) was 56.5 months, 75.2 months and 75.2 months for the intention-to-treat (ITT), HRD-positive and BRCA mutation-positive subpopulations, respectively (PMID: 37211045). The placebo cohort (n=269 [n=132, HRD-positive; n=80, BRCA mutation-positive; n=85, HRD-negative; n=52, HRD unknown]) demonstrated a median OS of 51.6 months (HR=0.92 [95% CI=0.76-1.12]; p=0.4118), 57.3 months (HR=0.62 [HR=0.45-0.85]) and 66.9 months (HR=0.60 [95% CI=0.39-0.93]) in the ITT, HRD-positive and BRCA mutation-positive subpopulations, respectively (PMID: 37211045).

Olaparib is an orally available, small molecule PARP inhibitor that is FDA-approved for patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious germline or somatic homologous recombination repair (HRR) gene-mutated metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (mCRPC), who have progressed following prior treatment with enzalutamide or abiraterone. RAD51C deleterious mutations for treatment with olaparib were detected by FoundationOne CDx or FoundationOne Liquid CDx. FDA approval was based on the results of the Phase III PROfound (NCT02987543) trial of olaparib versus enzalutamide or abiraterone acetate in adult patients with mCRPC who had disease progression while receiving a new hormonal agent and who had a deleterious or suspected deleterious mutation in a homologous recombination repair gene. 
In the Phase III PROfound (NCT02987543) trial, Cohort B (n=387 [BRCA2: n=127; ATM: n=84; BRCA1: n=13; CDK12: n=89; CHEK2: n=12; PPP2R2A: n=10; RAD51B: n=5; RAD54L: n=5; PALB2: n=4; BRIP1: n=3; CHEL1: n=2; BARD1: n=1; RAD515D: n=1; Co-occurring mutations: n=7]) treated with olaparib (n=256) demonstrated a median radiological progression-free survival (PFS) of 5.8 months (95% CI=5.5-7.4) (PMID: 32343890). In the enzalutamide or abiraterone acetate treated Cohort B (n=131), the median radiological PFS was 3.5 months (95% CI=2.2-3.7) (HR=00.49 [95% CI=0.38-0.63]; p<0.0001) (PMID: 32343890).

LEVEL_1.description:
Vismodegib is an orally available, small molecule SMO inhibitor that is FDA-approved for the treatment of adults with metastatic basal cell carcinoma (BCC), or with locally advanced BCC that has recurred following surgery or who are not candidates for surgery and who are not candidates for radiation. FDA approval was based on the Phase I SHH4476g (NCT00833417) trial of vismodegib in 96 patients with metastatic or locally advanced BCC. Gorlin syndrome, or nevoid BCC syndrome, is associated with germline PTCH1 mutations and predisposes individuals to the development of multiple BCCs, suggesting efficacy with vismodegib treatment as demonstrated in the Phase I SHH-4685s (NCT00957229) trial
In the Phase I SHH4476g (NCT00833417) trial, patients with metastatic BCC (n=33) demonstrated a median duration of response (DOR) of 7.6 months (range: 2.1-11.1), a median progression-free survival (PFS) of 9.5 months (95% CI=7.4-NE) and an independent review facility-confirmed objective response rate (ORR) of 30.3% (95% CI=15.6-48.2) (p=0.001), with a partial response (PR) rate of 30% (n=10) and stable disease (SD) rate of 64% (n=21) (PMID: 22670903). Patients with locally advanced BCC (n=63) demonstrated a median DOR of 7.6 months (range: 1.0-12.9), a median PFS of 9.5 months (95% CI=7.4-11.9) and an independent review facility-confirmed ORR of 42.9% (95% CI=30.5-56.0) (p<0.001), with a complete response (CR) rate of 20.6% (n=13), PR rate of 22.2% (n=14), SD rate of 38% (n=24) and progressive disease (PD) rate of 13% (n=8) (PMID: 22670903).
In the Phase I SHH-4685s (NCT00957229) trial of vismodegib versus placebo in patients with basal-cell nevus syndrome with germline PTCH1 mutations, the vismodegib cohort (n=26) demonstrated a median reduced size of existing surgically eligible BCCs of -71% versus -21% in the placebo cohort (n=15) (p=0.003) (PMID: 22670904). The vismodegib cohort had zero median number of surgeries per patient while the placebo cohort had 1.0 median number of surgeries per patient (p<0.001) (PMID: 22670904).

LEVEL_1.description:
Rucaparib is an orally available, small molecule PARP inhibitor that is FDA-approved for the maintenance treatment of adult patients with a deleterious BRCA mutation (germline and/or somatic)- associated recurrent epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal cancer who are in a complete or partial response to platinum-based chemotherapy. BRCA1 mutations for maintenance treatment with rucaparib were detected by the BRACAnalysis CDx or the FoundationFocus CDxBRCA Assay. FDA approval was based on the results of the Phase III ARIEL3 (NCT01968213) trial of rucaparib versus placebo in 564 patients with epithelial ovarian, fallopian tube, or primary peritoneal cancer. Additionally, rucaparib is NCCN recommended as a first-line maintenance treatment in newly diagnosed patients with advanced ovarian cancer based on the results of the Phase III ATHENA-MONO (NCT03522246) trial of rucaparib versus placebo in patients with newly diagnosed BRCA mutated ovarian cancer.

In the Phase III ARIEL3 (NCT01968213) trial, the rucaparib cohort (n=375 [BRCA1 mutated, n=80; BRCA2 mutated, n=50; Germline BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutated, n=82; Somatic BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutated, n=40]) (epithelial ovarian cancer, n=312; fallopian tube cancer, n=32; primary peritoneal cancer, n=31) demonstrated a median OS of 45.9 months (95% CI-37.7-59.6) and a median time to disease progression on subsequent therapy or death (PFS2) of 26.1 months (95% CI=22.8-32.8) in the BRCA-mutated subgroup (PMID: 40580808). In the placebo cohort (n=189 [BRCA1 mutated, n=37; BRCA2 mutated, n=29; Germline BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutated, n=48; Somatic BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutated, n=16]) (epithelial ovarian cancer, n=159; fallopian tube cancer, n=10; primary peritoneal cancer, n=19; high-grade serous adenocarcinoma, n=1), the median OS was 47.8 months. (95% CI=43.2-55.8) (HR=0.83 [95% CI=0.58-1.19]) and the median PFS2 was 18.2 months (95% CI=15.7-24.4) (HR=0.67 [95% CI=0.48-0.94]) in the BRCA-mutated subgroup (PMID: 40580808).

In the Phase III ATHENA-MONO (NCT03522246) trial, the homologous recombination deficiency (HRD) population was treated with either rucaparib (n=185 [BRCA mutated, n=91; BRCA wildtype/loss of heterozygosity [LOH] high, n=94] or placebo (n=49 [BRCA mutated, n=24; BRCA wildtype/LOH high, n=25] (PMID: 35658487). Of the rucaparib cohort, the median progression-free survival (PFS) was 28.7 months (95% CI=23.0-NR) and the objective response rate (ORR) was 58.8% (95% CI=32.9-81.6), with a 58.8% (n=10) partial response (PR) rate, 35.3% (n=6) stable disease (SD) rate and 5.9% (n=1) progressive disease (PD) rate (PMID: 35658487). Of the placebo cohort, the median PFS was 11.3 months (95% CI=9.1-22.1) (HR=0.47 [95% CI=0.31-22.1]; p=0.0004) and the ORR was 20.0% (95% CI=0.5-71.6), with a 20% (n=1) PR rate, 40% (n=2) SD rate and 40% (n=2) PD rate (PMID: 35658487).

LEVEL_1.description:
Olaparib is an orally available, small molecule PARP inhibitor that is FDA-approved in combination with abiraterone and prednisone or prednisolone for the treatment of adult patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious BRCA-mutated (BRCAm) metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer, as detected by an FDA-approved companion diagnostic. BRCA1 deleterious mutations for treatment with olaparib were detected by FoundationOne CDx or FoundationOne Liquid CDx. FDA approval was based on the results of the Phase III PROpel (NCT03732820) trial of olaparib plus abiraterone and prednisone/prednisolone in adult patients with mCRPC undergoing first-line treatment after failure of primary androgen deprivation therapy. 
In the Phase III PROpel (NCT03732820) trial, BRCAm patients (germline and somatic alterations) in the olaparib plus abiraterone and prednisone/prednisolone cohort (n=47) demonstrated a median radiological progression-free survival (rPFS) that was not reached (95% CI=NR-NR) and a median overall survival (OS) that not reached (95% CI=NR-NR) (PMID: 37714168). BRCAm patients in the placebo plus abiraterone and prednisone/prednisolone group (n=38) demonstrated a median rPFS of 8 months (95% CI=6-15)  (HR=0.24 [95% CI=0.12-0.45]) and a median OS of 23 months (95% CI=18-34) (HR=0.30 [95% CI=0.15-0.59]) (PMID: 37714168).

LEVEL_1.description:
Selumetinib is an orally available, small-molecule inhibitor of MEK1/2 that is FDA-approved for the treatment of adult patients and pediatric patients one year of age and older with neurofibromatosis type 1 (NF1) who have symptomatic, inoperable plexiform neurofibromas (PN). FDA approval for pediatric patients was based on the results of the Phase I/II SPRINT Stratum I (NCT01362803) trial of selumetinib in 50 pediatric patients with NF1 and inoperable PN. FDA approval for adult patients was based on the results of the Phase III KOMET (NCT04924608) trial of selumetinib versus placebo in 145 adult patients with NF1 and inoperable PN.
In the Phase II SPRINT Stratum I (NCT01362803) trial, patients (median age=10.2 years [range=3.5-17.4]) demonstrated an overall response rate (ORR) of 66% (n=33) (95% CI=51-79), with 33 confirmed partial responses (PR) (66%), and a median duration of response (DOR) that was not reached (95% CI=41.2-NE) (PMID: 32187457).
In the Phase III KOMET (NCT04924608) trial, the selumetinib cohort (n=71 [median age=31.0 years (IQR=24.0-40.0)]) demonstrated an ORR by the end of cycle sixteen (approximately fifteen months) of 20% (n=14) (95% CI=11.2-30.9), with fourteen (20%) PRs, 50 stable disease responses and one progressive disease response, and an observed DOR of at least six months in 86% of patients (PMID: 40473450). The placebo cohort (n=74 [median age=28.0 years (IQR=24.0-36.0)]) demonstrated an ORR by the end of cycle sixteen of 5% (n=4) (95% CI=1.5-13.3) (p=0.011), resulting in an estimated ORR difference of 14% (95% CI=3.8-25.8) (PMID: 40473450).

LEVEL_1.description:
Olaparib is an orally available, small molecule PARP inhibitor indicated in patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious germline-BRCA mutated, HER2-negative breast cancer human who have previously been treated with chemotherapy in the neoadjuvant, adjuvant or metastatic setting. FDA approval was based on the Phase III OlympiAD trial (NCT02000622) of single agent olaparib versus standard of care based on physician's choice in 302 patients with germline-BRCA mutated, HER2-negative metastatic breast cancer who had received no more than two previous chemotherapy regimens. FDA approval in the adjuvant setting for patients with germline-BRCA mutated high-risk early-stage breast cancer was based on the results of the Phase III OlympiA (NCT02032823) trial of olaparib versus placebo in patients with germline-BRCA mutated, HER2-negative early-stage breast cancer.

In the Phase III OlympiAD trial (NCT02000622), the olaparib cohort (n=205 [n=117, germline BRCA1 mutations; n=84, germline BRCA2 mutations; n=4, germline BRCA1/2 mutations])  demonstrated a median progression-free survival (PFS) of 7.0 months (95% CI=0.43-0.80), a confirmed objective response rate (ORR) of 59.9% (95%CI=52.0-67.4), and a complete response (CR) rate of 9.0% (PMID: 28578601). The standard therapy cohort (n=97 [n=51, germline BRCA1 mutations; n=46, germline BRCA2 mutations]) demonstrated a median PFS of 4.2 months (95% CI=0.43-0.80), a confirmed ORR of 28.8% (95%CI= 18.3-41.3), and a CR rate of 1.5%. Overall survival did not differ significantly between the groups (HR 0.90 [95%CI=0.63-1.29]) (PMID: 28578601).

In the Phase III OlympiA (NCT02032823) trial, the olaparib cohort (n=921 [n=657, germline BRCA1 mutations; n=261, germline BRCA2 mutations; n=2, germline BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutations; n=1, missing data]) had 85.9% of patients alive and free of invasive disease at three years versus 77.1% in the placebo cohort (n=915 [n=670, germline BRCA1 mutations; n=239, germline BRCA2 mutations; n=5, germline BRCA1 and BRCA2 mutations; n=1, missing data]) (difference=8.8% [95% CI=4.5-13.0]) (PMID: 34081848). Invasive disease-free survival was significantly longer in the olaparib cohort versus the placebo cohort (HR=0.58 [95% CI=0.41-0.821]; p<0.001) (PMID: 34081848).

LEVEL_1.description:
Belzutifan is an orally available, small molecule HIF-2α inhibitor that is FDA-approved for the treatment of adult patients with von Hippel-Lindau (VHL) disease who require therapy for associated renal cell carcinoma (RCC), central nervous system (CNS) hemangioblastomas, or pancreatic neuroendocrine tumors (pNET), not requiring immediate surgery. FDA approval was based on the Phase III LITESPARK-005 (NCT04195750) trial of belzutifan in 22 patients with pNET associated with VHL with a confirmed germine VHL alteration.  
In the Phase III LITESPARK-005 (NCT04195750) trial, the patients demonstrated a median duration of response that was not reached (range: (11.0+ to 37.3+), a median progression-free survival that was not reached (95% CI=NR-NR) and an overall response rate of 91% (95% CI=70.8–98.9), with a 32% (n=7) complete response rate, 59% (n=13) partial response rate and 9% (n=2) stable disease rate (PMID: 38393723).

Talazoparib is an orally available, small molecule PARP inhibitor that is FDA-approved for patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious germline BRCA-mutated, HER2-negative locally advanced or metastatic breast cancer. FDA approval was based on the results from the Phase III EMBRACA (NCT01945775) trial of talazoparib versus physician's choice (standard-therapy) in 431 patients with advanced, BRCA1/2 germline-mutated breast cancer.

In the Phase III EMBRACA (NCT01945775) trial, the talazoparib cohort (n=287 [n=133, germline BRCA1 mutations; n=154, germline BRCA2 mutations]) demonstrated a median progression-free survival (PFS) of 8.6 months (95% CI=7.2-9.3), a median overall survival (OS) of 22.3 months (95% CI=18.1-26.2) and an objective response rate (ORR) of 62.6% (95% CI=55.8-69.0), with a 5.5% (n=12) complete response (CR) rate, 57.1% (n=125) partial response (PR) rate and 21.0% (n=46) stable disease (SD) rate (PMID: 30110579). In the physician's choice cohort (n=144 [n=63, germline BRCA1 mutations; n=81, germline BRCA2 mutations]), the median PFS was 5.6 months (95% CI=4.2-6.7) (HR=0.54 [95% CI=0.41-0.71]; p<0.0001), the median OS was 19.5 months (95% CI=16.3-22.4) (HR=0.76 [95% CI=0.55-1.06]; p=0.105) and the ORR was 27.2% (95% CI=19.3-36.3), with a 27.2% (n=31) PR rate and 31.6% (n=36) SD rate (PMID: 30110579).

articles.title: Talazoparib in Patients with Advanced Breast Cancer and a Germline BRCA Mutation.
articles.journal: The New England journal of medicine
articles.reference: Litton JK et al. The New England journal of medicine. 2018 Aug 23;379(8)753-763.

LEVEL_1.description:
Olaparib is an orally available, small molecule PARP inhibitor that is FDA-approved in combination with abiraterone and prednisone or prednisolone for the treatment of adult patients with deleterious or suspected deleterious BRCA-mutated (BRCAm) metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer, as detected by an FDA-approved companion diagnostic. BRCA2 deleterious mutations for treatment with olaparib were detected by FoundationOne CDx or FoundationOne Liquid CDx. FDA approval was based on the results of the Phase III PROpel (NCT03732820) trial of olaparib plus abiraterone and prednisone/prednisolone in adult patients with mCRPC undergoing first-line treatment after failure of primary androgen deprivation therapy. 
In the Phase III PROpel (NCT03732820) trial, BRCAm patients (germline and somatic alterations) in the olaparib plus abiraterone and prednisone/prednisolone cohort (n=47) demonstrated a median radiological progression-free survival (rPFS) that was not reached (95% CI=NR-NR) and a median overall survival (OS) that not reached (95% CI=NR-NR) (PMID: 37714168). BRCAm patients in the placebo plus abiraterone and prednisone/prednisolone group (n=38) demonstrated a median rPFS of 8 months (95% CI=6-15)  (HR=0.24 [95% CI=0.12-0.45]) and a median OS of 23 months (95% CI=18-34) (HR=0.30 [95% CI=0.15-0.59]) (PMID: 37714168).

articles.title: Olaparib plus abiraterone versus placebo plus abiraterone in metastatic castration-resistant prostate cancer (PROpel): final prespecified overall survival results of a randomised, double-blind, phase 3 trial.
articles.journal: The Lancet. Oncology
articles.reference: Saad F et al. The Lancet. Oncology. 2023 Oct;24(10)1094-1108.
